Title: Emergence of lignin-carbohydrate interactions during plant stem maturation visualized by solid-state NMR

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 5-hydroxylated G-lignin precursors leading to S monolignols
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates coniferyl alcohol/coniferaldehyde at the C-5 position to generate precursors for syringyl monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for S-lignin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes S-lignin biosynthesis and strengthens lignin-carbohydrate packing; loss of FAH1 depletes S units, reduces lignin-carbohydrate contacts, and slightly compromises stem mechanical properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary wall strengthening, hydrophobicity, and stabilization of lignin-carbohydrate interfaces
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Solid-state NMR; solution NMR; lignin staining; mechanical testing; raw solid-state NMR deposited at Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.16904151
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stem maturation gradient; basal versus upper stem segments under standard hydroponic growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low-S fah1 mutant showed weakest lignin-carbohydrate cross-peak intensities and slightly reduced tensile strength/modulus
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering increased S-lignin proportion may improve lignin-carbohydrate interface properties without requiring higher total lignin; useful for biomass quality design
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell walls of inflorescence stem, especially interfascicular fibers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAH1 is required for S-lignin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis stems. 2. fah1-2 lacks detectable S monomers and shows predominantly G-lignin. 3. Loss of FAH1 markedly reduces lignin-carbohydrate contacts and is associated with slightly weaker mechanical properties, supporting an important role of S-lignin in wall stabilization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (fah1-2); solid-state NMR compositional and contact mapping; Mäule staining; tensile mechanical testing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on lignin composition and polymer interactions rather than direct enzymology; FAH1 function is interpreted through the established mutant phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamate to p-coumarate in the early phenylpropanoid pathway, affecting total lignin production and monolignol composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports overall lignin biosynthesis; ref3-3 mutation reduces total lignin content but increases S/G ratio, preserving strong lignin-carbohydrate packing and near-WT mechanical behavior
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary wall strengthening, hydrophobicity, and biomass structural properties
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Solid-state NMR; solution NMR; lignin staining; mechanical testing; raw solid-state NMR deposited at Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.16904151
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stem maturation gradient; basal stem analysis under standard hydroponic growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong reduction in overall lignin content, but no major mechanical penalty observed in tensile tests
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests lignin amount can potentially be reduced if favorable S/G composition and carbohydrate mixing are maintained, relevant to biomass processing optimization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell walls of inflorescence stem, with S-lignin prominent in fibers and G-lignin in vessels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. REF3/C4H acts upstream in lignin biosynthesis and its mutation lowers overall lignin content. 2. Despite reduced lignin amount, ref3-3 retains strong lignin-carbohydrate interactions because of a high S/G ratio. 3. These data show that molecular mixing patterns, rather than lignin abundance alone, are critical for stem wall properties.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (ref3-3); solid-state NMR compositional and contact mapping; Mäule staining; tensile mechanical testing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study uses ref3-3 as a compositionally altered lignin mutant to infer how lignin amount versus S/G ratio affects wall architecture.
